steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states Mrs. Sommers makes decisions "without conscious deliberation" — she acts on impulse, not reflection. The correct quotation must show her acting without thinking.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: Shows her reacting to what she observes — not acting without thought. ✗
  • Choice B: Shows her examining details carefully — deliberate attention, not impulse. ✗
  • Choice C — 'She was not thinking at all': Explicitly states she wasn't thinking, and describes surrender to "some mechanical impulse that directed her actions." Directly supports the claim. ✓
  • Choice D: Shows her observing and responding to surroundings — not unconscious action. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
